meta.name: DOCS Dimensional Obsessive-Compulsive Scale
meta.description: The DOCS is a 20-item self-report scale that assesses obsessive-compulsive symptom severity across four dimensions: contamination, responsibility for harm, unacceptable thoughts, and symmetry/ordering.
meta.author: Abramowitz JS, Deacon BJ, Olatunji BO, et al. (2010)
meta.reference: Abramowitz JS, Deacon BJ, Olatunji BO, et al. Assessment of obsessive-compulsive symptom dimensions. Psychol Assess. 2010;22(1):180-198.
meta.instruction: The following questions ask about four different types of concerns. Rate the degree to which you have experienced each aspect over the past month.

questions.text: How much time have you spent thinking about contamination (dirt, germs, chemicals) or feeling contaminated?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent contamination concerns?
If you had contamination thoughts but could not wash/clean, how distressed would you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by contamination concerns and washing/cleaning?
How difficult has it been to disregard contamination thoughts and refrain from washing/cleaning?
How much time have you spent thinking you might be responsible for something terrible happening?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent being responsible for harm?
If unable to check or get reassurance about potential harm, how distressed would you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by harm thoughts and checking/reassurance-seeking?
How difficult has it been to disregard harm-responsibility thoughts and refrain from checking?
How much time have you spent on unwanted intrusive thoughts (violent, sexual, immoral)?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent unwanted intrusive thoughts?
When you had unacceptable thoughts and could not perform a ritual, how distressed did you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by unacceptable thoughts and related rituals?
How difficult has it been to disregard unacceptable thoughts and refrain from rituals?
How much time have you spent on the need for symmetry, order, or balance?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent symmetry/order concerns?
If unable to arrange things to achieve symmetry, how distressed would you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by symmetry concerns and ordering?
How difficult has it been to disregard symmetry thoughts and refrain from ordering?

ranges.label: Minimal OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score indicates minimal obsessive-compulsive symptoms within the normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: No clinical concern at this time
Continue maintaining healthy thought patterns
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Mild OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Mild obsessive-compulsive symptoms that may cause some distress but are manageable. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Learn about OCD and intrusive thoughts
Practice accepting thoughts without rituals
Consider CBT-based self-help resources
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

ranges.label: Moderate OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Moderate symptoms likely affecting your daily functioning. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from an OCD specialist
Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) is gold-standard treatment
Review subscale scores to identify problematic dimensions
Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern

ranges.label: Severe OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Severe symptoms causing significant impairment in daily life, work, and relationships. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Please seek professional treatment as soon as possible
ERP therapy combined with medication may be most effective
OCD is a highly treatable condition
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support

contamination.label: Minimal
contamination.description: No significant contamination concerns. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

contamination.label: Mild
contamination.description: Mild contamination-related obsessions or compulsions. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

contamination.label: Moderate
contamination.description: Moderate contamination concerns affecting daily life. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

contamination.label: Severe
contamination.description: Severe contamination obsessions and washing compulsions. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties
